knowledges.label: gcn_1544d55444d743c8
knowledges.content: The momentum-resolved valence-band dispersion of single-crystalline orthorhombic CsPbBr$_3$ at room temperature was measured by angle-resolved photoelectron spectroscopy (ARPES); here the measured quasiparticle energy as a function of the in-plane crystal momentum was fitted near the valence-band maximum (VBM) by the isotropic in-plane parabolic dispersion E(k)=E_{\\mathrm{VBM}}+\\hbar^{2}k^{2}/(2m_{\\mathrm{exp}}), where k is the magnitude of the two-dimensional in-plane wave vector (units Å^{-1}), \\hbar is the reduced Planck constant, and m_e is the free electron mass, and the energy zero was set at the VBM defined as the energy of the maximum of the energy-distribution curve (EDC) at the band top; this fit, performed on the upper valence band along the surface high-symmetry direction labeled \\bar{\\Gamma}-\\bar{M}, returns an experimental hole effective mass m_{\\mathrm{exp}}=0.26\\pm0.02\\,m_e. [@Puppin2020]

metadata.lkm_original: The momentum-resolved valence-band dispersion of single-crystalline orthorhombic CsPbBr$_3$ at room temperature was measured by angle-resolved photoelectron spectroscopy (ARPES); here the measured quasiparticle energy as a function of the in-plane crystal momentum was fitted near the valence-band maximum (VBM) by the isotropic in-plane parabolic dispersion E(k)=E_{\\mathrm{VBM}}+\\hbar^{2}k^{2}/(2m_{\\mathrm{exp}}), where k is the magnitude of the two-dimensional in-plane wave vector (units Å^{-1}), \\hbar is the reduced Planck constant, and m_e is the free electron mass, and the energy zero was set at the VBM defined as the energy of the maximum of the energy-distribution curve (EDC) at the band top; this fit, performed on the upper valence band along the surface high-symmetry direction labeled \\bar{\\Gamma}-\\bar{M}, returns an experimental hole effective mass m_{\\mathrm{exp}}=0.26\\pm0.02\\,m_e.

knowledges.label: gcn_2e9e6e30a5d7431c
knowledges.content:
In an ARPES measurement of a crystalline semiconductor, let E(k) denote the measured quasiparticle energy as a function of the in-plane crystal momentum k (the magnitude of a two-dimensional wave vector measured in reciprocal angstroms, Å^{-1}), and let E_{\\mathrm{VBM}} denote the valence-band-maximum (VBM) energy defined as the highest occupied quasiparticle energy; within the momentum window used for fitting the upper valence band, taken here to be the region |k-k_{\\mathrm{VBM}}|\\lesssim0.1\\ \\text{\\AA}^{-1} around the momentum of the VBM, the quasiparticle dispersion is assumed to be well described by the isotropic in-plane parabolic form
      E(k)=E_{\\mathrm{VBM}}+\\frac{\\hbar^{2}k^{2}}{2m_{\\mathrm{exp}}},
      where \\hbar is the reduced Planck constant and m_{\\mathrm{exp}} is the fitted experimental hole effective mass; under this parabolic-band approximation, least-squares fitting of the experimentally extracted band maxima to this functional form returns m_{\\mathrm{exp}} and the systematic error in m_{\\mathrm{exp}} arising from neglected higher-order (nonparabolic) terms is smaller than the quoted experimental uncertainty of \\pm0.02\\,m_e, with m_e the free electron mass. [@Puppin2020]

metadata.lkm_original:
In an ARPES measurement of a crystalline semiconductor, let E(k) denote the measured quasiparticle energy as a function of the in-plane crystal momentum k (the magnitude of a two-dimensional wave vector measured in reciprocal angstroms, Å^{-1}), and let E_{\\mathrm{VBM}} denote the valence-band-maximum (VBM) energy defined as the highest occupied quasiparticle energy; within the momentum window used for fitting the upper valence band, taken here to be the region |k-k_{\\mathrm{VBM}}|\\lesssim0.1\\ \\text{\\AA}^{-1} around the momentum of the VBM, the quasiparticle dispersion is assumed to be well described by the isotropic in-plane parabolic form
      E(k)=E_{\\mathrm{VBM}}+\\frac{\\hbar^{2}k^{2}}{2m_{\\mathrm{exp}}},
      where \\hbar is the reduced Planck constant and m_{\\mathrm{exp}} is the fitted experimental hole effective mass; under this parabolic-band approximation, least-squares fitting of the experimentally extracted band maxima to this functional form returns m_{\\mathrm{exp}} and the systematic error in m_{\\mathrm{exp}} arising from neglected higher-order (nonparabolic) terms is smaller than the quoted experimental uncertainty of \\pm0.02\\,m_e, with m_e the free electron mass.
metadata.prior_justification: ARPES parabolic-fit assumption from LKM evidence chain; TODO:review

knowledges.label: gcn_0a71cf6357114b76
knowledges.content: Let an energy-distribution curve (EDC) be the ARPES-measured photoemission intensity as a function of binding energy E at fixed parallel momentum k; the energy position of the maximum of the measured EDC at the band top is used to define the valence-band-maximum energy E_{\\mathrm{VBM}} for the purposes of setting the energy zero and extracting an effective mass, and any systematic shift of the EDC peak maximum from the true quasiparticle band-edge caused by many-body interactions, inelastic background, or the instrumental lineshape is assumed to be smaller than the quoted energy/mass uncertainty so that using the EDC peak maximum to set E=0 does not induce a systematic error larger than \\pm0.02\\,m_e in the fitted hole effective mass. [@Puppin2020]

metadata.lkm_original: Let an energy-distribution curve (EDC) be the ARPES-measured photoemission intensity as a function of binding energy E at fixed parallel momentum k; the energy position of the maximum of the measured EDC at the band top is used to define the valence-band-maximum energy E_{\\mathrm{VBM}} for the purposes of setting the energy zero and extracting an effective mass, and any systematic shift of the EDC peak maximum from the true quasiparticle band-edge caused by many-body interactions, inelastic background, or the instrumental lineshape is assumed to be smaller than the quoted energy/mass uncertainty so that using the EDC peak maximum to set E=0 does not induce a systematic error larger than \\pm0.02\\,m_e in the fitted hole effective mass.
metadata.prior_justification: EDC peak-position assumption from LKM evidence chain; TODO:review

knowledges.label: gcn_e24a3d64aa604555
knowledges.content: ARPES intensity maps measured over in-plane momentum (k_x,k_y) and as a function of photon energy (which probes different values of the momentum component perpendicular to the surface, k_{\\perp}) together with band-unfolding calculations show that the observed periodicity of four valence-band maxima at the surface Brillouin-zone corners and the absence of an additional valence-band maximum at the surface \\bar{\\Gamma} point are not caused by surface reconstruction, k_{\\perp} dispersion, or matrix-element effects; specifically, energy- and polarization-dependent ARPES measurements reveal no signature of an additional VBM at \\bar{\\Gamma}, and unfolding of orthorhombic bands onto the cubic cell shows negligible spectral weight transfer to backfolded bands, so that the measured dispersion used to obtain m_{\\mathrm{exp}} represents bulk-like valence-band behavior relevant to the orthorhombic structure. [@Puppin2020]

metadata.lkm_original: ARPES intensity maps measured over in-plane momentum (k_x,k_y) and as a function of photon energy (which probes different values of the momentum component perpendicular to the surface, k_{\\perp}) together with band-unfolding calculations show that the observed periodicity of four valence-band maxima at the surface Brillouin-zone corners and the absence of an additional valence-band maximum at the surface \\bar{\\Gamma} point are not caused by surface reconstruction, k_{\\perp} dispersion, or matrix-element effects; specifically, energy- and polarization-dependent ARPES measurements reveal no signature of an additional VBM at \\bar{\\Gamma}, and unfolding of orthorhombic bands onto the cubic cell shows negligible spectral weight transfer to backfolded bands, so that the measured dispersion used to obtain m_{\\mathrm{exp}} represents bulk-like valence-band behavior relevant to the orthorhombic structure.
metadata.prior_justification: bulk-like ARPES dispersion assignment supported by measurements and unfolding in LKM chain; TODO:review
